{{Quote|I was a smuggler, way way back. I got shot down once over Ukatis.|Jolee Bindo|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Ukatis''' was an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] celestial body situated in the [[Ukatis system/Legends|Ukatis system]]. During the [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of the [[Star system/Legends|system]] at some point by [[3996 BBY]], the [[Ukatis enforcer]] [[Nayama]] shot down the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jolee Bindo]], forcing it to crash-land on Ukatis. Nayama then led Bindo to be [[Prisoner/Legends|incarcerated]] in a local [[Prison/Legends|prison]], from which he was subsequently broken out. Thereafter, the Padawan kidnapped Nayama, though the two later fell in love and would eventually [[Marriage/Legends|marry]] and leave Ukatis.

==Description==
Ukatis was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="KOTOR" /> located in the [[Ukatis system/Legends|Ukatis system]], a part of the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|At the time the Ukatis system was interdicted by its own King. ''[…]'' I found myself a ship and a partner and we began smuggling food and supplies to the Ukatis citizenry through the blockade. ''[…]'' We only got caught once. A lone Ukatish frigate shot us down and forced a crash landing.|Jolee Bindo|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:UkatisJediCivilWar-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|290px|By 3959 BBY, Ukatis was situated in the area widely explored by the Galactic Republic.]]
At some point by [[3996 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' establishes that [[Jolee Bindo]]'s [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was shot down over Ukatis at some point before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], which ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates to [[3996 BBY]].</ref> the [[Unidentified Ukatis system king|king]] of the Ukatis [[Star system/Legends|system]] ordered its [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in order to oppress the local citizens. Eventually, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Jolee Bindo]], after acquiring a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and a [[Jolee Bindo's smuggling partner|partner]], began [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and supplies through the blockade. At one point, the [[Ukatis enforcer]] [[Nayama]], operating aboard a lone Ukatish [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]], shot Bindo's craft down and forced it to crash-land on Ukatis.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The enforcer then dragged the Padawan to the local [[Capital/Legends|capital]], where he was [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]], and subsequently thwarted three of his escape attempts. Ultimately, Bindo was broken out of the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] and kidnapped Nayama. At some point thereafter, the two fell in love and [[Marriage/Legends|married]], Bindo [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] Nayama as a Jedi, and the two eventually traveled offworld.<ref name="KOTOR" /> By [[3959 BBY]], Ukatis fell within the "civilized [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]," the territory that had been widely [[Scout/Legends|explored]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TEA" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="3956 BBY">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' to [[3956 BBY]].</ref> Bindo relayed the story of his crash-landing on Ukatis to the Jedi trainee [[Revan/Legends|Revan]].<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
{{Quote|Nayama was a marvel of a woman. Fiery, determined, smart… she dragged me to the capital and foiled three of my attempts to escape prison.|Jolee Bindo|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
Ukatis was the site of a capital [[City/Legends|city]] that featured a prison.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ukatis was introduced in the [[2003]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. In the game, the celestial body can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, Revan, can have with Jolee Bindo. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion and that the conversation took place as described.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Ukatis system, and therefore Ukatis itself, in grid square L-15.<ref name="TEA" />
